quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hungarian paprika isn't just one spice—there are eight official grades ranging from mild and sweet to hot and pungent! The town of Szeged is so famous for its paprika that it's considered the paprika capital of the world, and the spice has been a cornerstone of Hungarian cuisine for over 300 years.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals in the Pacific Northwest
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals in the Strait of Juan de Fuca are remarkable swimmers who can dive up to 1,500 feet deep and hold their breath for nearly 30 minutes. Learn about the playful personalities and surprising behaviors of these charming local neighbors.

medium_read.title: The Hungarian Revolution of 1956: Voices of Freedom
medium_read.teaser: This moving account captures the courage of ordinary Hungarians who stood up for freedom during those dramatic October days. Through personal stories and historical photos, discover how this pivotal moment changed history and touched lives around the world.

long_read.title: Olympic National Park: A Wilderness Like No Other
long_read.teaser: From glacier-capped mountains to temperate rainforests and wild Pacific coastline, Olympic National Park contains an extraordinary diversity of ecosystems. This comprehensive guide explores what makes this corner of Washington so uniquely beautiful and how it continues to inspire visitors from around the world.

port_angeles.title: Fourth of July Weekend at the Waterfront
port_angeles.description: Tomorrow is Independence Day, and Port Angeles celebrates with a spectacular fireworks display over the harbor! The best views are from the City Pier and waterfront, where you can watch the colorful bursts reflect on the Strait of Juan de Fuca with the mountains as a backdrop. It's one of the most beautiful settings for July 4th anywhere in the country.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute while in flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right! That tiny heart is an incredible engine—beating up to 1,200 times per minute to power those amazing hovering abilities!
puzzle_1.close_response: Very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute in flight—that's 20 beats every second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Those tiny hearts are truly amazing—they beat about 1,200 times per minute while flying! That's what allows them to hover and fly backwards.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Hummingbirds have the fastest metabolism of all animals, and their hearts can slow down to 50 beats per minute when they enter torpor (a hibernation-like state) at night to conserve energy.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is indeed butterfly—such a lovely, musical word for such a delicate creature!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The word is 'pillangó'—isn't it beautiful? (Madár means bird, virág means flower, and méhecske means little bee.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language in Europe, unrelated to most other European languages. It's part of the Finno-Ugric language family and is known for its beautiful, musical quality and complex but logical structure.

puzzle_3.question: What type of tree is most common in the Hoh Rainforest in Olympic National Park?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Perfect! Sitka Spruce dominates the Hoh Rainforest, with some trees reaching over 300 feet tall—these ancient giants create the magical cathedral-like atmosphere of the rainforest!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's actually the Sitka Spruce that dominates the Hoh Rainforest! These magnificent trees can grow over 300 feet tall and live for 700 years, creating that enchanting, misty forest atmosphere.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The Hoh Rainforest receives 12-14 feet of rain annually, making it one of the wettest places in the continental United States. This incredible rainfall creates a lush, green wonderland draped in moss and ferns.
puzzle_3.learn_more_label: Visit the Hoh — National Park Service

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
